reasoning: Anthropic was designated as a supply chain risk under both 10 U.S.C. § 3252 and 41 U.S.C. § 4713 on March 4, 2026. While a California federal judge granted a preliminary injunction against the § 3252 designation on March 26, the § 4713 designation is subject to a separate legal challenge in the D.C. Circuit. Crucially, on April 8, 2026, the D.C. Circuit Court of Appeals denied Anthropic’s request for a preliminary injunction/stay regarding the § 4713 designation. This means that, as of April 12, the exclusion under § 4713 remains actively in effect. Given that May 1, 2026 is less than three weeks away, it is highly unlikely that Anthropic will be able to secure an emergency stay from the Supreme Court or reach a trial on the merits before that date. Therefore, the exclusion is very likely to still be in effect on the resolution date.
